Class Projectile

java.lang.Object
game.Projectile
public class Projectile extends Object
Models a single projectile with a three-state lifecycle: IDLE → AIMING → FLYING → IDLE. Handles parabolic physics, auto-oscillating aim angle, hit-box detection against both players, and a rolling trail buffer used for rendering.

Also serves as the single source of truth for all world-coordinate constants (GROUND_Y, start positions, hit-box bounds).

Physics (each FLYING frame)

   x  += vx
   y  += vy
   vy += GRAVITY

  • Constructor Details

    • Projectile

      public Projectile()

  • Method Details

    • startAiming

      public void startAiming(boolean player1Turn)
      Transitions to Projectile.State.AIMING for the given player's turn. Resets the angle to MIN_ANGLE unless locked (Cat perfect-aim).
      Parameters:
      player1Turn - true if it is P1's turn

    • fire

      public void fire()
      Transitions from Projectile.State.AIMING to Projectile.State.FLYING. Computes initial velocity from the current angle and player direction. Does nothing if state is not AIMING.

    • update

      public void update()
      Advances the projectile by one frame.
      • AIMING: oscillates angle unless locked.
      • FLYING: applies physics, appends trail point, checks hit boxes and out-of-bounds.

    • getTrajectoryPoints

      public double[][] getTrajectoryPoints(int count)
      Computes sample positions along the predicted parabolic arc for the aiming preview.
      Parameters:
      count - number of points to compute
      Returns:
      array of [x, y] pairs sampled every 3 frames

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Resets all state to IDLE and clears trail and offsets.

    • setLaunchOffset

      public void setLaunchOffset(double off)
      Parameters:
      off - horizontal offset applied to the shooter's start X (wind)

    • setTargetOffset

      public void setTargetOffset(double off)
      Parameters:
      off - horizontal offset applied to the target's hit box (wind)

    • setLockedAngle

      public void setLockedAngle(double a)
      Locks the launch angle (Cat's perfect-aim ability).
      Parameters:
      a - angle in degrees to lock to

    • clearAngleLock

      public void clearAngleLock()
      Releases the angle lock so oscillation resumes on the next startAiming(boolean) call.

    • getLaunchXOffset

      public double getLaunchXOffset()
      Returns:
      current launch X offset

    • isAngleLocked

      public boolean isAngleLocked()
      Returns:
      true if the angle is currently locked

    • getState

      public Projectile.State getState()
      Returns:
      current lifecycle state

    • isHit

      public boolean isHit()
      Returns:
      true if the last flight ended with a hit

    • getX

      public double getX()
      Returns:
      current X position in pixels

    • getY

      public double getY()
      Returns:
      current Y position in pixels

    • getAngle

      public double getAngle()
      Returns:
      current aiming angle in degrees

    • isPlayer1Turn

      public boolean isPlayer1Turn()
      Returns:
      true if the projectile belongs to P1's turn

    • getTrail

      public List<double[]> getTrail()
      Returns:
      the trail point list (each entry is [x, y])
